data.title: Disney's flagship sports app will be called 'ESPN' - CNBC
data.description:
The upcoming flagship sports streaming app by Disney (NYSE:DIS) will be simply called "ESPN," according to a CNBC report from Friday, citing sources.

The subscription is expected to cost $25 or $30 per month and the company will announce the name for the app next week at a media event.

The all-access sports service will consist of everything ESPN has to offer, including all games; programming on other ESPN cable networks such as ESPN2 and the SEC Network; ESPN on ABC; fantasy products; new betting tie-ins; studio programming; and documentaries, among other things, the report said.

The app, which is is expected to be a new distribution mechanism, will differ from ESPN’s current streaming product, ESPN+, which costs $11.99 per month. ESPN+ will remain a less expensive offering for consumers, sources told CNBC.

More on Disney

data.title: Snowflake shares log seven-day winning streak
data.description:
Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock ended 0.1% higher at $174.06 on Friday.

The information technology company gained 5.2% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has climbed 12.7% so far this year, compared to a 3.7% slip in the broader S&P 500 Index.

SNOW is up 15% over the past one month.

Snowflake has initiated proactive customer engagement practices in order to increase its net retention rate. Its largest customers also have healthier fundamentals and more stability, which makes the cloud data company better equipped to handle economic volatility, according to BTIG.

"Despite competition and macroeconomic risks, Snowflake's efficient gross profit generation and leadership in data warehousing support a careful buy recommendation," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, SNOW has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company received A in the prospect of profitability, A- in momentum, while it got a B+ growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 37 analysts gave SNOW a Buy and above rating. 12 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

data.title: Enbridge remains bullish on need for additional oil export capacity out of Western Canada
data.description:
Enbridge (NYSE:ENB) +0.6% in Friday's trading after beating Q1 adjusted earnings expectations, driven in part by higher earnings from its Mainline crude pipeline system - the largest in North America - which it said customers want to see expanded in spite of lower oil prices, Reuters reported.

Q1 net profit jumped to C$2.26B, or C$1.04/share, from C$1.42B, or C$0.67/share, in the year-ago quarter, while distributed cash flow rose to C$3.78B from C$3.46B a year earlier, as the company attributed strong utilization across its asset base that positions it to meet or exceed financial guidance for the year.

Enbridge (NYSE:ENB) said the Mainline system was full to capacity for all of Q1, delivering a Q1 record of 3.2M bbl/day and a rise in Q1 adjusted EBITDA to C$1.45M from C$1.34B in the year-earlier quarter.

The company has forecast the need for ~1M bbl/day of additional pipeline space out of Western Canada by 2035 as oil sands operators increase production.

Enbridge (ENB) expects to make a final investment decision on the first phase of its Mainline expansion project that would add 150K bbl/day of capacity to the network later this year, CEO Greg Ebel said on the company's earnings conference call.

The CEO said Enbridge's (ENB) bullish stance on the need for additional oil pipeline capacity in North America has not changed, in spite of weaker oil prices, as "those shorter-term swings, which we watch very closely, can have some impact on production but they don't change the long-term view of energy demand and need," Reuters reported.

Ebel also said he is "completely enthused" by how governments in both Canada and the U.S. are recognizing the need to build oil and gas projects more quickly, noting "the fact that people are now talking about energy, energy infrastructure, getting stuff done - that's just a marked change here in Canada."

"We don't expect tariffs to have a material impact on our current operations or deployment of capital," Ebel also said.

data.title: Information services group targets $60.5M Q2 revenues amid strong AI demand
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Information Services Group, Inc. (III) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Michael Connors, Chairman and CEO, highlighted robust Q1 results with revenues of $60 million, reflecting a 5% year-over-year increase excluding divested automation unit revenues. Growth was primarily driven by the Americas region, which grew 17% year-over-year, marking its largest quarterly growth in two years.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 rose 68% to $7.4 million, with a margin expansion of over 550 basis points to 12.4%, attributed to disciplined operating practices and an improved business mix.
  • AI continued to play a significant role, with the company serving over 200 clients with AI-focused services in the last 12 months, up from 150 in Q4 2024. ISG Tango, the company’s sourcing platform, recorded $9 billion in contract value, a 30% increase from the prior quarter.
  • Recurring revenues reached $26 million in Q1, representing 44% of total revenues, slightly up from Q4 2024.
  • CFO Michael Sherrick reported net income of $1.5 million, or $0.03 per diluted share, compared to a net loss of $3.4 million in Q1 2024. Adjusted net income was $3.7 million, or $0.07 per share.

Outlook

  • For Q2 2025, the company provided revenue guidance in the range of $59.5 million to $60.5 million and adjusted EBITDA between $7 million and $8 million.
  • Connors noted strong demand in Q2 driven by cloud, AI, and data analytics, while maintaining a conservative outlook amidst ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty.
  • Europe is expected to see growth in the second half of 2025, supported by improving pipelines in cost optimization and AI-related services.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 revenues of $59.6 million reflected a 5% year-over-year growth when excluding contributions from the divested automation unit.
  • Regional revenue performance included $41 million from the Americas (up 17%), $13.8 million from Europe (down 13%), and $4.8 million from Asia-Pacific (down 15%).
  • Consulting utilization reached 77.7%, compared to 70.2% in Q1 2024.
  • The company ended the quarter with $20.1 million in cash, with $1 million in net cash generated from operations during the quarter. Share repurchases totaled $3.3 million, and $15 million remains authorized for future buybacks.

Q&A

  • Dave Storms, Stonegate, inquired about the strong Americas performance and expectations for Q2. Connors indicated continued double-digit growth in Q2, driven by strong demand across transformation and optimization services.
  • Marc Riddick, Sidoti, asked about utilization and hiring needs. CFO Sherrick stated that utilization is at the high end of their comfort range and hiring remains aligned with demand.
  • Vincent Colicchio, Barrington Research, questioned the AI strategy. Connors explained that nearly all engagements now include AI components, and the company is continuously training its workforce to meet increasing demand.
  • Joseph Gomes, Noble Capital Markets, sought clarity on AI client expansion. Connors highlighted the rapid increase in AI engagements and projected further scaling as clients transition to broader AI adoption.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment regarding the company’s Americas performance and AI-driven growth. Questions were generally constructive, focusing on growth opportunities and operational strengths.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing disciplined practices, strong market positioning, and AI-centered strategies, while acknowledging macroeconomic uncertainties, particularly in Europe and Asia-Pacific.
  • Compared to Q4 2024, management exhibited increased optimism about sustained demand and margin improvements, particularly in the Americas and AI segments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The Americas region showed accelerated growth from 6% in Q4 2024 to 17% in Q1 2025. Utilization also improved from 72% to 77.7%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margins expanded significantly from 11.3% in Q4 2024 to 12.4% in Q1 2025.
  • Recurring revenue remained flat quarter-over-quarter, while AI-driven revenues and ISG Tango platform activity showed notable increases.
  • Management tone shifted from cautious optimism in Q4 2024 to a more confident outlook for 2025, particularly regarding the Americas and AI-related services.

Risks and Concerns

  • European and Asia-Pacific markets remain subdued due to geopolitical uncertainties and delayed government spending, respectively.
  • The macroeconomic environment, including tariff impacts and geopolitical instability, continues to pose potential risks to client spending and project pipelines.
  • Management cited the need for a cautious approach in guidance due to these external factors.

Final Takeaway

Information Services Group delivered a strong Q1 2025, driven by double-digit growth in the Americas and a sharp rise in AI-related services. The company’s focus on AI-centered strategies and disciplined operations has enhanced profitability and positioned it well for sustained demand in cloud, AI, and digital transformation services. While challenges persist in Europe and Asia-Pacific, management remains optimistic about continued growth, particularly in the U.S. market.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: US Steel gains amid reports Nippon Steel will meet with Trump officials next week
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) reached highs of the day, rising 2.9%, amid reports that officials from Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) will meet with Trump administration officials next week.

Nippon Steel Vice President Takahiro Mori is scheduled to meet with the Trump officials in Washington as a deadline for the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States approaches, according to a  posting on X from a Semafor reporter. Mori will also visit Pennsylvania.

The $14 billion planned sale of US Steel (NYSE:X) was delayed in January when former President Joe Biden blocked the combination on national security grounds. President Trump began his term noting that he "wouldn't mind" if Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) took a minority stake in US Steel (NYSE:X) but has repeatedly said a foreign company should not control US Steel (NYSE:X).

US Steel (NYSE:X) jumped 16% on April 7 after Trump ordered a national security review of its planned sale to Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY). The new review may allow the Trump administration to potentially approve the $55 a share sale of US Steel (X).

News of the Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) visit to the U.S. was reported by CTFN on Thursday.

data.title: Opal fuels anticipates 37% RNG production growth for 2025 amid strong segment performance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: OPAL Fuels Inc. (OPAL) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Co-CEO Adam Comora highlighted that Q1 2025 results were aligned with expectations, with adjusted EBITDA reaching $20.1 million, marking a 30% year-over-year increase. The Fuel Station Services segment showed significant growth with EBITDA of $12.5 million, reflecting an 80% annual increase. RNG fuel production reached 1.1 million MMBtu, up nearly 40% from the prior year.
  • The company reaffirmed its full-year guidance set in March, expecting sequential RNG production growth throughout 2025, driven by ramping of new projects and expansion at existing facilities.
  • Co-CEO Comora acknowledged uncertainties in the macroeconomic and regulatory environments but noted that recent regulatory shifts could positively impact RNG adoption for heavy-duty trucking.
  • CFO Kazi Hasan stated that Q1 revenue was $85.4 million, up from $64.9 million in Q1 2024. Net income increased to $1.3 million from $0.7 million year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA from equity method investments was $3.4 million for the quarter.
  • CFO Hasan confirmed a liquidity position of $240 million as of March 31, which includes $40 million in cash and equivalents and over $178 million in undrawn credit facilities, supporting ongoing capital plans and growth initiatives.

Outlook

  • RNG production guidance for 2025 was reiterated at 5.0 million to 5.4 million MMBtu, representing a midpoint increase of 37% compared to 2024.
  • Fuel Station Services is anticipated to grow adjusted EBITDA by 30%-50% in 2025, supported by new station builds and increasing throughput.
  • Co-CEO Comora mentioned that regulatory clarity is expected soon, with potential implications for RNG adoption and pricing.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2025 was $85.4 million, compared to $64.9 million in Q1 2024. Adjusted EBITDA grew to $20.1 million, up from $15.2 million in the previous year.
  • Net income rose to $1.3 million, compared to $0.7 million in Q1 2024, reflecting operational improvements and production ramp-ups.
  • Capital expenditures for Q1 totaled $17 million, including $5.4 million related to equity method investments.
  • The company expects to generate $50 million from investment tax credit sales in 2025, bolstering cash flow.

Q&A

  • Derrick Whitfield, Texas Capital: Questioned the production growth trajectory and operational improvements. Co-CEO Jonathan Maurer explained that Q1 production was affected by cold weather and virtual pipeline availability but expressed confidence in sequential growth due to operational enhancements and project ramp-ups.
  • Matthew Blair, TPH: Asked about RIN pricing dynamics and Fuel Station Services growth. Co-CEO Comora noted that Q1 RIN prices averaged $2.71 but are expected to be lower in Q2. He attributed Fuel Station Services growth to increased station builds, throughput, and higher utilization.
  • Martin Malloy, Johnson Rice & Company: Inquired about plans for returning capital to shareholders. Co-CEO Comora emphasized the company's flexibility in deploying free cash flow for growth projects, M&A, or shareholder returns, depending on market conditions.
  • Adam Bubes, Goldman Sachs: Sought clarity on the timeline for new regulatory outcomes. Co-CEO Comora indicated that clarity on 45Z tax credits and RFS rules is expected within the next few months.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism about production growth and regulatory clarity, with queries focused on timelines and growth drivers. Their tone was largely neutral but proactive in seeking details about potential risks and opportunities.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and responses, emphasizing strategic execution, growth prospects, and alignment with regulatory developments. Phrases like “we remain confident” and “we are positioned well” underscored their outlook.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, with a focus on execution and regulatory engagement.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Financial guidance remained unchanged, with RNG production and EBITDA projections consistent with prior expectations.
  • The Fuel Station Services segment showed stronger growth momentum, with an 80% year-over-year increase in Q1 EBITDA.
  • Analysts' focus shifted slightly towards regulatory outcomes and their impact on growth trajectories, reflecting evolving industry dynamics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Regulatory uncertainties, including clarity on 45Z tax credits and EPA rulings, were highlighted as potential risks.
  • Management noted delays in investment decisions among logistics and trucking customers due to trade policy uncertainties, although these were not deemed material.
  • Co-CEO Comora acknowledged the need for resolution on tariffs and international market access to unlock additional opportunities.

Final Takeaway

OPAL Fuels demonstrated solid financial performance and operational growth in Q1 2025, driven by increased RNG production and strong contributions from the Fuel Station Services segment. The company remains on track to achieve its full-year guidance, with a focus on sequential production growth and regulatory clarity to support long-term adoption of RNG solutions. Management’s confident outlook, supported by robust liquidity and disciplined execution, positions OPAL Fuels well for continued growth in 2025.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ttec expands AI-enabled solutions and offshore operations for profitability growth in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TTEC Holdings, Inc. (TTEC) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ken Tuchman highlighted that revenue reached $534 million with EBITDA increasing to $56 million, driven by improved margins. He noted that the economic environment remains challenging, causing caution among clients, but emphasized the company's progress in signing new clients and expanding AI-enabled solutions.
  • Tuchman stated that TTEC's partnerships with hyperscalers are central to its strategy, with investments in proprietary AI capabilities and digital transformation solutions strengthening its market position.
  • CFO Kenny Wagers shared that the adjusted EBITDA margin improved to 10.6%, reflecting the impact of operational efficiencies and stronger revenue retention. He confirmed that the company's financial performance aligns with full-year guidance expectations.
  • Tuchman also mentioned that TTEC Engage achieved profitability improvements through an expanded offshore footprint and AI integration, while TTEC Digital focused on enterprise-wide digital transformations.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated the 2025 full-year guidance, citing confidence in profitability improvements despite macroeconomic uncertainties.
  • Wagers indicated that second-half results are expected to show higher profitability, supported by new enterprise client wins and growth in offshore business. He noted that revenue from these new clients is forecasted to grow significantly year-over-year.
  • Tuchman emphasized the company's focus on AI and digital transformation, predicting it will drive meaningful contributions to long-term growth.

Financial Results

  • Revenue was reported at $534 million, a 7.4% decline year-over-year, attributed to challenges in the macroenvironment. EBITDA improved to $56 million, reflecting operational efficiencies and cost control measures.
  • TTEC Engage generated $426 million in revenue with profitability improvements from offshore growth and AI-enabled solutions. TTEC Digital reported $108 million in revenue, a decline driven by reduced one-time product sales but offset by growth in recurring managed services.
  • The company reported free cash flow of $16 million, marking a $45 million year-over-year improvement due to better operating cash flow and reduced capital expenditures.

Q&A

  • George Sutton, Craig-Hallum, asked about client adoption challenges and AI reliability. Tuchman explained that clients are cautious due to macro uncertainties and the need to understand AI capabilities, but TTEC is positioned to benefit from these longer-term opportunities.
  • Maggie Nolan, William Blair, inquired about differentiation through hyperscaler partnerships. Tuchman highlighted TTEC's expertise in CX transformation and AI, underlining its unique focus compared to broader industry players.
  • Cassie Chan, Bank of America, asked about cost optimization efforts. Tuchman and Wagers discussed ongoing initiatives to expand offshore capabilities and leverage AI for operational efficiencies.
  • Vijay Devar, Northland Capital Markets, questioned deal delays. Wagers confirmed no significant delays but noted smaller deal sizes in the current environment.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about macroeconomic uncertainties and client caution but acknowledged TTEC's progress in operational efficiencies and AI investments.
  • Management maintained a positive tone, emphasizing confidence in long-term growth through strategic initiatives and new client wins. Tuchman consistently showed optimism about the company's positioning in the CX market.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more focused on addressing economic challenges and highlighting the benefits of AI and offshore expansion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue declined compared to Q4 2024, but EBITDA margins improved, demonstrating operational efficiency gains.
  • Guidance language remained cautious due to macro uncertainties, aligning with the prior quarter's outlook.
  • Management continued to emphasize diversification and AI-driven solutions as key strategic priorities.
  • Analysts maintained a consistent focus on macro trends and client behavior.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified client caution and economic uncertainty as ongoing challenges.
  • Analysts raised concerns about smaller deal sizes and potential delays in client adoption of new technologies.
  • The company is mitigating these risks through diversification, AI investments, and operational cost optimization.

Final Takeaway

TTEC Holdings' Q1 2025 earnings call highlighted operational improvements and strategic investments in AI and offshore capabilities to drive profitability. Despite macroeconomic uncertainties, the company reiterated its full-year guidance and emphasized strong client acquisition momentum and partnerships with hyperscalers. Management's focus on technology-driven solutions and cost efficiencies positions TTEC for long-term growth and resilience in the evolving CX market.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Santander rejected bid of £11B from NatWest for UK unit - FT
data.description:
Banco Santander (NYSE:SAN) rejected an offer of ~ £11bn for its UK retail bank from NatWest (NWG) earlier this year.

The Spanish bank rejected the offer as too low, and the bid is no longer around, according to a Financial Times report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

NatWest (NWG) made an offer for Santander UK unit that was higher than £10bn but lower than £12bn, according to the report. 

“As we have said, the UK is not for sale and is a core part of Santander’s diversified business model," Santander told the FT.

NatWest declined to comment to the FT. 

The FT first reported in February that NatWest approached Banco Santander (NYSE:SAN) about purchasing the UK unit.
